concurrency_note: check_traceability is a single-pass static text scan of epic docs + a checked-in source tree at a fixed SHA — there is no concurrent/racing dimension in the artifact to evade.

authority_boundary.proves: every stable-ID contract/invariant carries a code @cw-trace guards/ensures annotation (coverage) and a test/probe/policy/telemetry @cw-trace verifies annotation (tested), every BR has an incoming realizes from a defined contract/invariant, and every @cw-trace annotation targets a defined ID with a schema-valid verb/source-kind pairing — over the file extensions the scanner walks (.py/.go/.ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx/.java/.rb/.rs/.rego/.yaml/.yml)
authority_boundary.artifact: a git worktree copy of the target repo's epic docs (docs/epics/<slug>) plus its tracked source tree
assumptions: an @cw-trace annotation is trusted as accurate by presence — the checker verifies the tag exists, not that the annotated code/test actually implements the guard/verification it claims to
only annotations that appear as literal text on a single line are found; a dynamically-constructed or multi-line-split annotation is invisible to the regex
only the listed file extensions are scanned; an annotation placed in an unscanned extension (e.g. .txt, .md outside the epic doc tree) does not count toward coverage

seeded_defect_trials.injected: order.py: removed the `@cw-trace guards CTR-order-001 INV-order-003` annotation entirely
test_create_order.py: guards annotation left intact, but the verifies annotation silently dropped INV-order-003 (kept only CTR-order-001)
order.py: the real create_order lost its guards annotation, but a decoy `@cw-trace guards CTR-order-001 INV-order-003` was placed on an unrelated no-op function that does not implement the guard
test_create_order.py lost its real verifies annotation; a decoy verifies annotation for both IDs was placed in notes.txt, an extension outside SOURCE_EXTS
